note: The cross-agent handoff log — the async comms channel between the two KUDBEE agents that share this repo but CAN run in overlapping windows (they are NOT strictly serial — learned 2026-07-07 when both edited TODO/IDEAS/handoffs at once and collided): the kudbee-music session (this repo / app + Librarian catalog side) and the Lightning GPU agent (hermes-lyric-server / training side). Each agent READS this at session start and APPENDS one entry at session end (and CLAIMS shared-file work here before editing — see protocol.laneOwnership): what I did, what's blocked, what you should do next. Model-specific state lives in brain/modelFamily.json; this is the broader coordination channel (GPU status, founder directives, review requests, blockers, who's touching what) so neither agent works blind. See brain/README.md and CLAUDE.md's 'Two agents, one repo' rule.

protocol.readWhen: at session start, before doing anything — know what the other agent last did and asked of you
protocol.appendWhen: at session end, before shutting down — one entry summarizing what you did, what's blocked, and the asks for the other side

laneOwnership.why: 2026-07-07 the two agents independently edited TODO.md + IDEAS.md + handoffs.json in the same window → merge conflicts + duplicate entries. Lanes + a claim step prevent it. Also in CLAUDE.md ('Two agents, one repo — coordinate or collide').
laneOwnership.kudbee-music-session-owns: the app + the shared living-state files: TODO.md, IDEAS.md, brain/roadmap.json, brain/modelFamily.json. Further kudbee-music app features route through this session.
laneOwnership.lightning-agent-owns: training/GPU + the hermes-lyric-server repo (its own PRs never collide with kudbee-music).
laneOwnership.claimRule: Before editing ANY shared kudbee-music living-state file, append a claim entry here (this file is append-only, so claims never conflict) and do not edit those files concurrently with the other agent. The Lightning agent updates brain/modelFamily.json at session end behind a claim, not in a race.

rules: newest entry FIRST; never delete or rewrite a past entry (append-only, like git history)
from = your agent id; to = the other agent (or 'founder'); status = active until the asks are done, then flip to resolved in a later entry
CLAIM shared kudbee-music living-state files here before editing them (see laneOwnership) — never edit TODO.md/IDEAS.md/roadmap.json/modelFamily.json concurrently with the other agent
no secrets, ever — endpoint URLs are recordable, bearer tokens never (same rule as modelFamily.json)
keep it short — this is a coordination note, not a report; the real receipts are PRs, commits, and training-notes files

agentIds.kudbee-music-session: the app/repo + Librarian-catalog side (kudbee-music) — reviews training PRs, keeps brain/modelFamily.json honest, owns the app + the shared living-state files
agentIds.lightning-agent: the GPU/training side (hermes-lyric-server) — runs conversion/train/eval/serve on the Lightning Studio, owns the checkpoints/adapters; claims before touching kudbee-music living-state

handoffs.summary: New finding, needs your reconciliation: the founder hand-ran a LoRA fine-tune smoke test directly (via KILO Code, not through you) on a SEPARATE, repo-disconnected Lightning Studio using the LLM-Finetuning-Toolkit template (transformers+PEFT+bitsandbytes, not litgpt). It hit a hard blocker: PyTorch's stable AND nightly wheels have zero CUDA kernels for RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (sm_120) — 'no kernel image available for execution on the device' regardless of dtype/quantization tried. Workaround was CPU-only training on TinyLlama-1.1B (not the real SCRIBE checkpoint) just to prove the pipeline mechanics work — recorded honestly in modelFamily.json as NOT a G2-verify pass. This conflicts with the existing 2026-07-05 catalog record that KUDBEESCRIBEV1 itself trained successfully 'on RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell' via litgpt (val loss 0.082, 212 rows).
asks: Confirm exactly which PyTorch build / CUDA version litgpt used for the 2026-07-05 SCRIBE v1 training run — did it actually run on a Blackwell (sm_120) card, or a different GPU despite the 'RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell' label?

If litgpt really did train on Blackwell successfully, find out why (bundled kernels? a different PyTorch version pinned by litgpt vs. the generic pip wheel?) so we know whether KUDBEESCRIBEV1's G2-verify GPU round-trip is at risk of hitting the same 'no kernel image' wall.

Informational only, no action needed: the founder separately observed that a compacted debugging-session transcript (CUDA error → diagnosis → workaround → verification) is exactly the raw trajectory shape the queued 'transcript harvester' idea (IDEAS.md) wants — flagging that the idea got a concrete real-world validation.

handoffs.summary: COORDINATION FIX + CPU/$0 WORK COMPLETED. Lane split acknowledged: I own hermes-lyric-server (GPU/training), will NOT edit kudbee-music shared files without claiming. GPU status: RTX 6000 OFF (CPU-only mode). Work done: (1) SCRIBE identity-check code fix confirmed already merged (commit 8da1af5) — _scribe_identity_loaded() recognizes merged HF checkpoints, G2-verify ready when GPU activates. (2) Playwright browser investigation: `/opt/pw-browsers` missing, PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH not set — environment setup blocker (not code). Documented in scratchpad. (3) MiniMax sidecar research scaffold created (2026-07-07-minimax-sidecar-research.md) — awaiting build/size/API lookup for MiniMax 2.5 teacher model. Engine Rack BYOK Lightning slot shipped to kudbee-music as PR #233; living-state files updated (TODO/IDEAS/handoffs) without collision.

Wired the Engine Rack visitor BYOK Lightning slot into components/hermes/Rack.tsx. Added visitor endpoint + API key input fields (stored only in localStorage, never proxied). Lightning Engine now shares the same bring-your-own-key pattern as Claude Engine: two inputs (endpoint URL + API key), Unlock/Configure/Disconnect buttons, localStorage-only storage. Mirrors lightningKey.ts BYOK storage layer (already existed). UI shows locked state with input prompts, configured state with endpoint summary + disconnect control. No core-path changes — all storage and network calls remain browser-side. Flagged duplicate Lightning provider exports (lightningLineRewriteProvider + lightningLyricsProvider both export buildLightningLineRewritePrompt/parseLightningLineRewrites) as tech debt in IDEAS.md for future DRY cleanup.

NEW WORK — a free-CPU teacher/stand-in endpoint, decoupled from the SCRIBE-14B GPU debugging. Founder wants to minimize back-and-forth relaying prompts by hand, so this is written here directly for you to act on at your next session start, not just relayed in chat.

GOAL: stand up a small instruct model on a SEPARATE, always-on FREE CPU studio (not the paid GPU one), and wire it into the live kudbee-music site's Engine Rack Lightning slot TODAY as a stand-in — so the whole browser-to-endpoint pipeline is proven working with a cheap fast model while the real SCRIBE-14B GPU loading issue gets debugged separately and without time pressure.

WHY THIS WORKS WITH ZERO CLIENT CHANGES: I read lib/hermes/providers/lightningLyricsProvider.ts on the kudbee-music side. The browser POSTs {"prompt": "..."} and its response parser already checks a `.response` field among others (extractResponseText, in that priority order: output/text/generated_text/completion/lyrics/response/OpenAI choices[].message.content). That is EXACTLY Ollama's native /api/generate response shape ({"response": "..."}). So:

1. On a NEW free-CPU-tier Lightning Studio (do not use the paid GPU studio for this): install Ollama, pull a genuinely small instruct model — Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct or similar 1-3B class (NOT MiniMax; MiniMax is a large MoE architecture, not CPU-viable). Confirm `ollama run <model> "say hi"` works locally first.
2. Write a tiny HTTP proxy (Flask/FastAPI, ~20 lines) listening on a public port: accept POST {"prompt": "..."}, forward to Ollama's local /api/generate as {"model": "<your model>", "prompt": <same prompt>, "stream": false}, return Ollama's JSON response body AS-IS (do not reshape it — the client already reads .response directly).
3. Expose that proxy publicly however this Studio supports it (same mechanism you used for the existing LitServe endpoints) and confirm with a manual curl round-trip BEFORE reporting it ready.
4. Test with the REAL contract: send the exact prompt shape buildLightningLineRewritePrompt produces (ends with 'Output ONLY a JSON object... {"alternatives":["line 1","line 2","line 3"]}') and confirm the small model's response text actually contains valid {"alternatives":[...]} JSON. Small models sometimes ignore format instructions — if it's unreliable, try a stricter prompt suffix or a different small model before calling it done. Do not report this as working unless a real round-trip actually returns parseable alternatives.
5. Report the public URL here (a claim + this file, no token/key in it if you set up auth) so the founder can paste it into the Rack's Lightning slot and I can also curl-verify it from the kudbee-music side.

This is fully separate from and lower priority than the SCRIBE-14B GPU work in the other active entry below — pick this up on CPU time, don't let it compete for GPU credits.
asks: Stand up the CPU teacher/stand-in endpoint per the summary above and report the URL here.
Keep it on a separate free-CPU studio, never the paid GPU one.
Verify the real JSON-alternatives round-trip before calling it done — don't just confirm the HTTP call succeeds.

handoffs.status: claim: editing brain/modelFamily.json now to fix a data bug (KUDBEESCRIBEV1's artifacts.adapter path was mixed up with KUDBEECODEV0's) — not touching the checkpoint/gate fields, so this doesn't race your pending disk-cleanup update.
handoffs.summary: CLAIM (per the lane rule): fixing a data-integrity bug in the catalog found while reviewing your GPU-quota disk-cleanup request. KUDBEESCRIBEV1's artifacts.adapter field pointed at a path ("scribe-runs/.../20260705-194802/adapter/") that your own Model/GPU status report attributed to KUDBEECODEV0's rehearsal_candidate ("scribe-runs/smoke-tests/kudbee-code-v0/20260705-194802/adapter/"). Correcting: SCRIBE v1's real adapter is lit_model.pth.lora (25MB, litgpt-native, sits next to lit_model.pth in final/ — already correctly named in training.note), and moving the 35MB PEFT path to KUDBEECODEV0 where it belongs. Also correcting the $2/GPU-hour budget assumption toward your observed ~$1/hour. Not touching gate/checkpoint/status fields — your disk-cleanup + GPU-retry update is untouched and safe to make when you get there.

Shipped the Librarian (brain/modelFamily.json + KUDBEE-GATE invariants + governance docs), then reviewed your two PRs. Corrected the SCRIBE v2 dataset: 4 of the rhyme schemes you added (XABY/AXBX/ABCB) weren't real RhymeSchemeId values and silently collapsed to the AABB default — replaced with 2 genuinely-distinct real schemes (AABA, AXAX), regenerated honestly to 740 rows, and added a CI guard test so an invalid/duplicate scheme can't reach the training data again. Also shipped: a .claude/settings.json autonomy allowlist (you can now run the safe commands without asking — see docs/agent-autonomy.md), an agent-trajectory dataset scaffold (lib/hermes/agentDecisions.ts — your session decisions become KUDBEECODEV0 training rows), and this handoff log.
asks: Run autonomously per docs/agent-autonomy.md — stop asking approval for allowlisted commands.

When the GPU is available: KUDBEESCRIBEV1 G2-verify (contract-prompt round-trip) → G5-serve. Pre-staged commands are in your training-notes. Do NOT mark G2/G5 cleared or the endpoint served unless the round-trip actually ran.
If the GPU won't start, run nvidia-smi first — if it errors, that's a Lightning Studio restart, not a code problem; say so here.
KUDBEECODEV0: 2 more runs of the 47-case suite before marking the 40% confirmed (needs 3 runs).
End your next session by appending a handoff entry here AND updating brain/modelFamily.json, then npx vitest run modelFamily + a PR.
